text.title: compaction-preserve-refs Failure Modes
text.summary: Three failure modes that destroy the resume invariant. Each has a symptom, root cause, and fix.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Compacting via "summarize the conversation in 200 words" with no schema.
antipattern.reason: The model invents the schema each call; references survive only by accident, and downstream tooling cannot rely on key names being stable.

section.p: Symptom: next session reads the compact and asks the user to restate the task. Root cause: free-form summary prompt. Fix: replace the prompt with the YAML-schema template from `templates/compaction-prompt.txt` and validate the output via `scripts/validate-compaction-preserve-refs.py`.

antipattern.description: Letting the LLM add ad-hoc keys ("notes", "thoughts", "todo") that drift between calls.
antipattern.reason: Schema drift means orchestrators can't validate; downstream tooling that expects `errors_open` breaks when the model writes `open_errors` or buries the field under `notes`.

section.p: Symptom: KeyError in the next stage's loader. Root cause: prompt does not list "additionalProperties: false". Fix: set `additionalProperties: false` in the JSON Schema and re-emit the prompt with the explicit "no other keys" clause.

antipattern.description: Paraphrasing file paths or error messages "for readability" ("login.py around line 40", "the test failed").
antipattern.reason: The compacted state is a manifest of pointers; paraphrasing breaks the dereference. The next session searches for a path that does not exist or an error string that no longer matches.

section.p: Symptom: next session opens the wrong file or claims the bug is fixed when it is not. Root cause: prompt says "summarize" instead of "preserve verbatim". Fix: replace verbs in the prompt with "preserve verbatim" and add the regex-style anchors from `templates/compaction-prompt.txt`.

antipattern.description: Including the full body of a tool output (test log, search response) inside the compacted state.
antipattern.reason: The point of compaction is to free context. Embedding tool output defeats the purpose and squeezes the surviving references out of the budget.

section.p: Symptom: compacted state exceeds the per-message budget on next load. Root cause: prompt did not enforce "drop tool output bodies". Fix: drop the bodies, keep only the `refs` entry pointing at the on-disk log path.

antipattern.description: Emitting `next_action:` as an empty string or a vague sentence ("continue", "keep going").
antipattern.reason: Resume requires a concrete first imperative. Without it, the next session loses minutes choosing where to start, often picking the wrong subtask.

section.p: Symptom: next session asks "what now?". Root cause: prompt allows empty strings. Fix: validator rejects empty `next_action`; prompt requires "one imperative sentence naming a file and verb".
